description:
tests/concurrent_writes.rs::parallel_bl_create_across_workers_all_succeed intermittently fails under 'cargo tarpaulin' (the make check / pre-commit coverage gate): one worker's task is missing from 'bl list' (observed 2026-05-21: 'list missing parallel-4', 7 of 8 tasks present). It passes deterministically under plain 'cargo test' (5/5 standalone re-runs) — tarpaulin's llvm-instrumented, slower binary widens a real race window in concurrent 'bl create' against the balls/tasks state branch.

Not introduced by bl-022c (whose only runtime change is a pure Config::validate check). Pre-existing; surfaced while running the coverage gate for bl-022c. Related to the env-race family already fixed under bl-bfa8 / bl-ad4b but this one is a state-branch write race, not an env race.

Impact: make check and the pre-commit hook fail randomly for everyone. Either harden the concurrent-create commit path (retry/lock on the state-branch index) or make the test tolerant of tarpaulin timing.
title: Flaky test: parallel_bl_create_across_workers_all_succeed races under tarpaulin
